| 名称=Sanuki Shrine | 画像=[[File:Sanuki-jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013|1=Kitakatsuragi District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Kōryō|Q1346830|WD=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}}328 Miyoshi | 位置={{Coord|34|33|16|N|135|44|21|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Mikoto|1=Kunitama}}
Waka-uka-no-me-no-Mikoto
{{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 別名=Hirose Daimyōjin / Minami Kawai Myōjin | 本殿=sangen-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=hiwadabuki|1=UNKNOWN}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} | 神事= | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Sanuki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sanuki Shrine''' (さぬきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Miyoshi, {{ill|lt=Kōryō|Q1346830|WD=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013|1=Kitakatsuragi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Because the "old bamboo cutter" in '{{ill|lt=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|Q923593|WD=Q923593|1=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter}}' is named "Sanuki no Miyatsuko," the shrine presents itself as one related to the tale, identifying this with Sanuki in Hirose District of Yamato{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Kōryō Town (main volume) |date=2001-5-31 |publisher=Kōryō Town |pages=1125-1139 |author=Kōryō Town}}. === Enshrined deities === Currently it enshrines {{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Mikoto|1=Kunitama}}, Waka-uka-no-me-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}. In '{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}},' entry for {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 7 ({{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355|1=883}}), 12th month, 2nd day, it says "bestow Junior Fifth Lower rank on Sankichi Ōtate-no-Mikoto and Sankichi Inoki deity," thought to refer to this shrine's deities. === History === The place name "Miyoshi" is read "Mitsuyoshi" now, but it was formerly written "Sankichi" and read "Sanuki." This area is thought to have been settled by the {{ill|lt=Imibe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229|lt=Imibe clan|1=Inbe clan}} of {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}, who invited the deity of their homeland and founded the shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Sanuki Shrine, Hirose District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}," ranked as a small shrine. Because Waka-uka-no-me-no-Mikoto of Hirose Kawai (present {{ill|lt=Hirose Taisha|Q705281|WD=Q705281|1=Hirose Taisha}}) was invited, until the Edo period it was called "Hirose Daimyōjin" and "Minami Kawai Myōjin." A fire occurred in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1611|Q6768|WD=Q6768|1=1611}}), first month; the current honden was rebuilt at that time. === Precinct === File:讃岐神社本殿.jpg|Honden File:Sanuki-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii ファイル:讃岐神社境内出土 石製坩 (J-13065・13066).JPG|Stone crucibles excavated in precinct
{{small|{{ill|lt=Tokyo National Museum|Q653433|WD=Q653433|1=Tokyo National Museum}} display.}} === Sanuki Shrine and The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter === There are multiple theories about the setting of '{{ill|lt=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|Q923593|WD=Q923593|1=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter}}' (the Kaguya-hime legend), often called Japan's oldest narrative, and Kōryō in Kitakatsuragi District, Nara Prefecture, is said to be a strong candidate. '''Reasons Kōryō is said to be the setting''' * The old bamboo cutter's name is '''Sanuki no Miyatsuko''' - opening of the tale: "Long ago there was an old bamboo cutter ... his name was called Sanuki no Miyatsuko." * The old man's name indicates he was the head of the Sanuki lineage living in the Yamato locality called '''Sankichi (Sanuki)'''. * In the Heian-period "Engishiki," the location of the Shikinaisha Sanuki Shrine is written as '''Sankichi-gō, Hirose District, Yamato Province''', corresponding to '''Miyoshi, Kōryō, Kitakatsuragi District, Nara'''. === Film location === [[ファイル:奈良県_広陵町_讃岐神社_ロケ地表示.jpg|サムネイル|Sanuki Shrine filming-location sign]] It was selected as one filming location for the movie '{{ill|lt=Tenshi no Iru Toshokan|Q28943614|WD=Q28943614|1=Library with Angels}},' set in Nara's Katsuragi area ({{ill|lt=Yamatotakada|Q838669|WD=Q838669|1=Yamatotakada, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kashiba|Q696643|WD=Q696643|1=Kashiba, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672|1=Katsuragi, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kōryō|Q1346830|WD=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}}){{Cite web|url=http://www.toshokan-movie.com/|title=Japanese film "Tenshi no Iru Toshokan"|access-date=2017-2-11}}. Starring: {{ill|lt=Koshiba Fūka|Q11463509|WD=Q11463509|1=Fuka Koshiba}}; co-starring: {{ill|lt=Yokohama Ryūsei|Q15876471|WD=Q15876471|1=Ryusei Yokohama}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related item === {{Commonscat|Sanuki-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Umami Kofun Group|Q11668062|WD=Q11668062|1=Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さぬきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sanuki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sanuki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:広陵町の歴史]] [[Category:広陵町の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:忌部氏|社さぬきしんしや]][[Category:qq]]

In '{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}},' entry for {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 7 ({{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355|1=883}}), 12th month, 2nd day, it says "bestow Junior Fifth Lower rank on Sankichi Ōtate-no-Mikoto and Sankichi Inoki deity," thought to refer to this shrine's deities. === History === The place name "Miyoshi" is read "Mitsuyoshi" now, but it was formerly written "Sankichi" and read "Sanuki." This area is thought to have been settled by the {{ill|lt=Imibe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229|lt=Imibe clan|1=Inbe clan}} of {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}, who invited the deity of their homeland and founded the shrine. A fire occurred in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1611|Q6768|WD=Q6768|1=1611}}), first month; the current honden was rebuilt at that time. === Precinct === File:讃岐神社本殿.jpg|Honden File:Sanuki-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii ファイル:讃岐神社境内出土 石製坩 (J-13065・13066).JPG|Stone crucibles excavated in precinct
{{small|{{ill|lt=Tokyo National Museum|Q653433|WD=Q653433|1=Tokyo National Museum}} display.}} === Sanuki Shrine and The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter === There are multiple theories about the setting of '{{ill|lt=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|Q923593|WD=Q923593|1=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter}}' (the Kaguya-hime legend), often called Japan's oldest narrative, and Kōryō in Kitakatsuragi District, Nara Prefecture, is said to be a strong candidate. '''Reasons Kōryō is said to be the setting''' * The old bamboo cutter's name is '''Sanuki no Miyatsuko''' - opening of the tale: "Long ago there was an old bamboo cutter ... his name was called Sanuki no Miyatsuko." * The old man's name indicates he was the head of the Sanuki lineage living in the Yamato locality called '''Sankichi (Sanuki)'''. * In the Heian-period "Engishiki," the location of the Shikinaisha Sanuki Shrine is written as '''Sankichi-gō, Hirose District, Yamato Province''', corresponding to '''Miyoshi, Kōryō, Kitakatsuragi District, Nara'''. === Film location === [[ファイル:奈良県_広陵町_讃岐神社_ロケ地表示.jpg|サムネイル|Sanuki Shrine filming-location sign]] It was selected as one filming location for the movie '{{ill|lt=Tenshi no Iru Toshokan|Q28943614|WD=Q28943614|1=Library with Angels}},' set in Nara's Katsuragi area ({{ill|lt=Yamatotakada|Q838669|WD=Q838669|1=Yamatotakada, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kashiba|Q696643|WD=Q696643|1=Kashiba, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672|1=Katsuragi, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kōryō|Q1346830|WD=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}}){{Cite web|url=http://www.toshokan-movie.com/|title=Japanese film "Tenshi no Iru Toshokan"|access-date=2017-2-11}}. Starring: {{ill|lt=Koshiba Fūka|Q11463509|WD=Q11463509|1=Fuka Koshiba}}; co-starring: {{ill|lt=Yokohama Ryūsei|Q15876471|WD=Q15876471|1=Ryusei Yokohama}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related item === {{Commonscat|Sanuki-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Umami Kofun Group|Q11668062|WD=Q11668062|1=Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sanuki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sanuki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:広陵町の歴史]] [[Category:広陵町の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:忌部氏|社さぬきしんしや]][[Category:qq]]

| 名称=Sanuki Shrine | 画像=[[File:Sanuki-jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013|1=Kitakatsuragi District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Kōryō|Q1346830|WD=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}}328 Miyoshi | 位置={{Coord|34|33|16|N|135|44|21|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Mikoto|1=Kunitama}}
Waka-uka-no-me-no-Mikoto
{{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 別名=Hirose Daimyōjin / Minami Kawai Myōjin | 本殿=sangen-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=hiwadabuki|1=UNKNOWN}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} | 神事= | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Sanuki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sanuki Shrine''' (さぬきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Miyoshi, {{ill|lt=Kōryō|Q1346830|WD=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013|1=Kitakatsuragi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Because the "old bamboo cutter" in '{{ill|lt=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|Q923593|WD=Q923593|1=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter}}' is named "Sanuki no Miyatsuko," the shrine presents itself as one related to the tale, identifying this with Sanuki in Hirose District of Yamato{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Kōryō Town (main volume) |date=2001-5-31 |publisher=Kōryō Town |pages=1125-1139 |author=Kōryō Town}}. === Enshrined deities === Currently it enshrines {{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Mikoto|1=Kunitama}}, Waka-uka-no-me-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}. In '{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}},' entry for {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 7 ({{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355|1=883}}), 12th month, 2nd day, it says "bestow Junior Fifth Lower rank on Sankichi Ōtate-no-Mikoto and Sankichi Inoki deity," thought to refer to this shrine's deities. === History === The place name "Miyoshi" is read "Mitsuyoshi" now, but it was formerly written "Sankichi" and read "Sanuki." This area is thought to have been settled by the {{ill|lt=Imibe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229|lt=Imibe clan|1=Inbe clan}} of {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}, who invited the deity of their homeland and founded the shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Sanuki Shrine, Hirose District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}," ranked as a small shrine. Because Waka-uka-no-me-no-Mikoto of Hirose Kawai (present {{ill|lt=Hirose Taisha|Q705281|WD=Q705281|1=Hirose Taisha}}) was invited, until the Edo period it was called "Hirose Daimyōjin" and "Minami Kawai Myōjin." A fire occurred in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1611|Q6768|WD=Q6768|1=1611}}), first month; the current honden was rebuilt at that time. === Precinct === File:讃岐神社本殿.jpg|Honden File:Sanuki-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii ファイル:讃岐神社境内出土 石製坩 (J-13065・13066).JPG|Stone crucibles excavated in precinct
{{small|{{ill|lt=Tokyo National Museum|Q653433|WD=Q653433|1=Tokyo National Museum}} display.}} === Sanuki Shrine and The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter === There are multiple theories about the setting of '{{ill|lt=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|Q923593|WD=Q923593|1=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter}}' (the Kaguya-hime legend), often called Japan's oldest narrative, and Kōryō in Kitakatsuragi District, Nara Prefecture, is said to be a strong candidate. '''Reasons Kōryō is said to be the setting''' * The old bamboo cutter's name is '''Sanuki no Miyatsuko''' - opening of the tale: "Long ago there was an old bamboo cutter ... his name was called Sanuki no Miyatsuko." * The old man's name indicates he was the head of the Sanuki lineage living in the Yamato locality called '''Sankichi (Sanuki)'''. * In the Heian-period "Engishiki," the location of the Shikinaisha Sanuki Shrine is written as '''Sankichi-gō, Hirose District, Yamato Province''', corresponding to '''Miyoshi, Kōryō, Kitakatsuragi District, Nara'''. === Film location === [[ファイル:奈良県_広陵町_讃岐神社_ロケ地表示.jpg|サムネイル|Sanuki Shrine filming-location sign]] It was selected as one filming location for the movie '{{ill|lt=Tenshi no Iru Toshokan|Q28943614|WD=Q28943614|1=Library with Angels}},' set in Nara's Katsuragi area ({{ill|lt=Yamatotakada|Q838669|WD=Q838669|1=Yamatotakada, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kashiba|Q696643|WD=Q696643|1=Kashiba, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672|1=Katsuragi, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kōryō|Q1346830|WD=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}}){{Cite web|url=http://www.toshokan-movie.com/|title=Japanese film "Tenshi no Iru Toshokan"|access-date=2017-2-11}}. Starring: {{ill|lt=Koshiba Fūka|Q11463509|WD=Q11463509|1=Fuka Koshiba}}; co-starring: {{ill|lt=Yokohama Ryūsei|Q15876471|WD=Q15876471|1=Ryusei Yokohama}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related item === {{Commonscat|Sanuki-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Umami Kofun Group|Q11668062|WD=Q11668062|1=Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sanuki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sanuki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:広陵町の歴史]] [[Category:広陵町の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:忌部氏|社さぬきしんしや]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 別名=Hirose Daimyōjin / Minami Kawai Myōjin | 本殿=sangen-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=hiwadabuki|1=UNKNOWN}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} | 神事= | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Sanuki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sanuki Shrine''' (さぬきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Miyoshi, {{ill|lt=Kōryō|Q1346830|WD=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013|1=Kitakatsuragi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Because the "old bamboo cutter" in '{{ill|lt=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|Q923593|WD=Q923593|1=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter}}' is named "Sanuki no Miyatsuko," the shrine presents itself as one related to the tale, identifying this with Sanuki in Hirose District of Yamato{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Kōryō Town (main volume) |date=2001-5-31 |publisher=Kōryō Town |pages=1125-1139 |author=Kōryō Town}}. === Enshrined deities === Currently it enshrines {{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Mikoto|1=Kunitama}}, Waka-uka-no-me-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}. In '{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}},' entry for {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 7 ({{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355|1=883}}), 12th month, 2nd day, it says "bestow Junior Fifth Lower rank on Sankichi Ōtate-no-Mikoto and Sankichi Inoki deity," thought to refer to this shrine's deities. === History === The place name "Miyoshi" is read "Mitsuyoshi" now, but it was formerly written "Sankichi" and read "Sanuki." This area is thought to have been settled by the {{ill|lt=Imibe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229|lt=Imibe clan|1=Inbe clan}} of {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}, who invited the deity of their homeland and founded the shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Sanuki Shrine, Hirose District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}," ranked as a small shrine. Because Waka-uka-no-me-no-Mikoto of Hirose Kawai (present {{ill|lt=Hirose Taisha|Q705281|WD=Q705281|1=Hirose Taisha}}) was invited, until the Edo period it was called "Hirose Daimyōjin" and "Minami Kawai Myōjin." A fire occurred in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1611|Q6768|WD=Q6768|1=1611}}), first month; the current honden was rebuilt at that time. === Precinct === File:讃岐神社本殿.jpg|Honden File:Sanuki-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii ファイル:讃岐神社境内出土 石製坩 (J-13065・13066).JPG|Stone crucibles excavated in precinct
{{small|{{ill|lt=Tokyo National Museum|Q653433|WD=Q653433|1=Tokyo National Museum}} display.}} === Sanuki Shrine and The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter === There are multiple theories about the setting of '{{ill|lt=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|Q923593|WD=Q923593|1=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter}}' (the Kaguya-hime legend), often called Japan's oldest narrative, and Kōryō in Kitakatsuragi District, Nara Prefecture, is said to be a strong candidate. '''Reasons Kōryō is said to be the setting''' * The old bamboo cutter's name is '''Sanuki no Miyatsuko''' - opening of the tale: "Long ago there was an old bamboo cutter ... his name was called Sanuki no Miyatsuko." * The old man's name indicates he was the head of the Sanuki lineage living in the Yamato locality called '''Sankichi (Sanuki)'''. * In the Heian-period "Engishiki," the location of the Shikinaisha Sanuki Shrine is written as '''Sankichi-gō, Hirose District, Yamato Province''', corresponding to '''Miyoshi, Kōryō, Kitakatsuragi District, Nara'''. === Film location === [[ファイル:奈良県_広陵町_讃岐神社_ロケ地表示.jpg|サムネイル|Sanuki Shrine filming-location sign]] It was selected as one filming location for the movie '{{ill|lt=Tenshi no Iru Toshokan|Q28943614|WD=Q28943614|1=Library with Angels}},' set in Nara's Katsuragi area ({{ill|lt=Yamatotakada|Q838669|WD=Q838669|1=Yamatotakada, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kashiba|Q696643|WD=Q696643|1=Kashiba, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672|1=Katsuragi, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kōryō|Q1346830|WD=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}}){{Cite web|url=http://www.toshokan-movie.com/|title=Japanese film "Tenshi no Iru Toshokan"|access-date=2017-2-11}}. Starring: {{ill|lt=Koshiba Fūka|Q11463509|WD=Q11463509|1=Fuka Koshiba}}; co-starring: {{ill|lt=Yokohama Ryūsei|Q15876471|WD=Q15876471|1=Ryusei Yokohama}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related item === {{Commonscat|Sanuki-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Umami Kofun Group|Q11668062|WD=Q11668062|1=Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sanuki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sanuki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:広陵町の歴史]] [[Category:広陵町の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:忌部氏|社さぬきしんしや]][[Category:qq]]

Sanuki-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県北葛城郡広陵町三吉328', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sanuki-jinja}} [[JA:讃岐神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11632552}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Sanuki Shrine | 画像=[[File:Sanuki-jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013|1=Kitakatsuragi District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Kōryō|Q1346830|WD=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}}328 Miyoshi | 位置={{Coord|34|33|16|N|135|44|21|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Mikoto|1=Kunitama}}
Waka-uka-no-me-no-Mikoto
{{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 別名=Hirose Daimyōjin / Minami Kawai Myōjin | 本殿=sangen-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=hiwadabuki|1=UNKNOWN}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} | 神事= | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Sanuki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sanuki Shrine''' (さぬきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Miyoshi, {{ill|lt=Kōryō|Q1346830|WD=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013|1=Kitakatsuragi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Because the "old bamboo cutter" in '{{ill|lt=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|Q923593|WD=Q923593|1=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter}}' is named "Sanuki no Miyatsuko," the shrine presents itself as one related to the tale, identifying this with Sanuki in Hirose District of Yamato{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Kōryō Town (main volume) |date=2001-5-31 |publisher=Kōryō Town |pages=1125-1139 |author=Kōryō Town}}. === Enshrined deities === Currently it enshrines {{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Mikoto|1=Kunitama}}, Waka-uka-no-me-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}. In '{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}},' entry for {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 7 ({{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355|1=883}}), 12th month, 2nd day, it says "bestow Junior Fifth Lower rank on Sankichi Ōtate-no-Mikoto and Sankichi Inoki deity," thought to refer to this shrine's deities. === History === The place name "Miyoshi" is read "Mitsuyoshi" now, but it was formerly written "Sankichi" and read "Sanuki." This area is thought to have been settled by the {{ill|lt=Imibe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229|lt=Imibe clan|1=Inbe clan}} of {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}, who invited the deity of their homeland and founded the shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Sanuki Shrine, Hirose District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}," ranked as a small shrine. Because Waka-uka-no-me-no-Mikoto of Hirose Kawai (present {{ill|lt=Hirose Taisha|Q705281|WD=Q705281|1=Hirose Taisha}}) was invited, until the Edo period it was called "Hirose Daimyōjin" and "Minami Kawai Myōjin." A fire occurred in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1611|Q6768|WD=Q6768|1=1611}}), first month; the current honden was rebuilt at that time. === Precinct === File:讃岐神社本殿.jpg|Honden File:Sanuki-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii ファイル:讃岐神社境内出土 石製坩 (J-13065・13066).JPG|Stone crucibles excavated in precinct
{{small|{{ill|lt=Tokyo National Museum|Q653433|WD=Q653433|1=Tokyo National Museum}} display.}} === Sanuki Shrine and The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter === There are multiple theories about the setting of '{{ill|lt=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|Q923593|WD=Q923593|1=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter}}' (the Kaguya-hime legend), often called Japan's oldest narrative, and Kōryō in Kitakatsuragi District, Nara Prefecture, is said to be a strong candidate. '''Reasons Kōryō is said to be the setting''' * The old bamboo cutter's name is '''Sanuki no Miyatsuko''' - opening of the tale: "Long ago there was an old bamboo cutter ... his name was called Sanuki no Miyatsuko." * The old man's name indicates he was the head of the Sanuki lineage living in the Yamato locality called '''Sankichi (Sanuki)'''. * In the Heian-period "Engishiki," the location of the Shikinaisha Sanuki Shrine is written as '''Sankichi-gō, Hirose District, Yamato Province''', corresponding to '''Miyoshi, Kōryō, Kitakatsuragi District, Nara'''. === Film location === [[ファイル:奈良県_広陵町_讃岐神社_ロケ地表示.jpg|サムネイル|Sanuki Shrine filming-location sign]] It was selected as one filming location for the movie '{{ill|lt=Tenshi no Iru Toshokan|Q28943614|WD=Q28943614|1=Library with Angels}},' set in Nara's Katsuragi area ({{ill|lt=Yamatotakada|Q838669|WD=Q838669|1=Yamatotakada, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kashiba|Q696643|WD=Q696643|1=Kashiba, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672|1=Katsuragi, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kōryō|Q1346830|WD=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}}){{Cite web|url=http://www.toshokan-movie.com/|title=Japanese film "Tenshi no Iru Toshokan"|access-date=2017-2-11}}. Starring: {{ill|lt=Koshiba Fūka|Q11463509|WD=Q11463509|1=Fuka Koshiba}}; co-starring: {{ill|lt=Yokohama Ryūsei|Q15876471|WD=Q15876471|1=Ryusei Yokohama}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related item === {{Commonscat|Sanuki-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Umami Kofun Group|Q11668062|WD=Q11668062|1=Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sanuki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sanuki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:広陵町の歴史]] [[Category:Architecture of Koryō Town]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:忌部氏|社さぬきしんしや]][[Category:qq]]

'''Reasons Kōryō is said to be the setting''' * The old bamboo cutter's name is '''Sanuki no Miyatsuko''' - opening of the tale: "Long ago there was an old bamboo cutter ... his name was called Sanuki no Miyatsuko." * The old man's name indicates he was the head of the Sanuki lineage living in the Yamato locality called '''Sankichi (Sanuki)'''. * In the Heian-period "Engishiki," the location of the Shikinaisha Sanuki Shrine is written as '''Sankichi-gō, Hirose District, Yamato Province''', corresponding to '''Miyoshi, Kōryō, Kitakatsuragi District, Nara'''. === Film location === [[ファイル:奈良県_広陵町_讃岐神社_ロケ地表示.jpg|サムネイル|Sanuki Shrine filming-location sign]] It was selected as one filming location for the movie '{{ill|lt=Tenshi no Iru Toshokan|Q28943614|WD=Q28943614|1=Library with Angels}},' set in Nara's Katsuragi area ({{ill|lt=Yamatotakada|Q838669|WD=Q838669|1=Yamatotakada, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kashiba|Q696643|WD=Q696643|1=Kashiba, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672|1=Katsuragi, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kōryō|Q1346830|WD=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}}){{Cite web|url=http://www.toshokan-movie.com/|title=Japanese film "Tenshi no Iru Toshokan"|access-date=2017-2-11}}. Starring: {{ill|lt=Koshiba Fūka|Q11463509|WD=Q11463509|1=Fuka Koshiba}}; co-starring: {{ill|lt=Yokohama Ryūsei|Q15876471|WD=Q15876471|1=Ryusei Yokohama}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related item === {{Commonscat|Sanuki-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Umami Kofun Group|Q11668062|WD=Q11668062|1=Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sanuki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sanuki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:Architecture of Koryō Town]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:忌部氏|社さぬきしんしや]][[Category:qq]]

Sanuki-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県北葛城郡広陵町三吉328', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sanuki-jinja}} [[JA:讃岐神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11632552}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Sanuki Shrine | 画像=[[File:Sanuki-jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013|1=Kitakatsuragi District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Kōryō|Q1346830|WD=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}}328 Miyoshi | 位置={{Coord|34|33|16|N|135|44|21|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Mikoto|1=Kunitama}}
Waka-uka-no-me-no-Mikoto
{{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 別名=Hirose Daimyōjin / Minami Kawai Myōjin | 本殿=sangen-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=hiwadabuki|1=UNKNOWN}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} | 神事= | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Sanuki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sanuki Shrine''' (さぬきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Miyoshi, {{ill|lt=Kōryō|Q1346830|WD=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013|1=Kitakatsuragi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Because the "old bamboo cutter" in '{{ill|lt=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|Q923593|WD=Q923593|1=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter}}' is named "Sanuki no Miyatsuko," the shrine presents itself as one related to the tale, identifying this with Sanuki in Hirose District of Yamato{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Kōryō Town (main volume) |date=2001-5-31 |publisher=Kōryō Town |pages=1125-1139 |author=Kōryō Town}}. === Enshrined deities === Currently it enshrines {{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Mikoto|1=Kunitama}}, Waka-uka-no-me-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}. In '{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}},' entry for {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 7 ({{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355|1=883}}), 12th month, 2nd day, it says "bestow Junior Fifth Lower rank on Sankichi Ōtate-no-Mikoto and Sankichi Inoki deity," thought to refer to this shrine's deities. === History === The place name "Miyoshi" is read "Mitsuyoshi" now, but it was formerly written "Sankichi" and read "Sanuki." This area is thought to have been settled by the {{ill|lt=Imibe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229|lt=Imibe clan|1=Inbe clan}} of {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}, who invited the deity of their homeland and founded the shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Sanuki Shrine, Hirose District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}," ranked as a small shrine. Because Waka-uka-no-me-no-Mikoto of Hirose Kawai (present {{ill|lt=Hirose Taisha|Q705281|WD=Q705281|1=Hirose Taisha}}) was invited, until the Edo period it was called "Hirose Daimyōjin" and "Minami Kawai Myōjin." A fire occurred in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1611|Q6768|WD=Q6768|1=1611}}), first month; the current honden was rebuilt at that time. === Precinct === File:讃岐神社本殿.jpg|Honden File:Sanuki-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii ファイル:讃岐神社境内出土 石製坩 (J-13065・13066).JPG|Stone crucibles excavated in precinct
{{small|{{ill|lt=Tokyo National Museum|Q653433|WD=Q653433|1=Tokyo National Museum}} display.}} === Sanuki Shrine and The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter === There are multiple theories about the setting of '{{ill|lt=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|Q923593|WD=Q923593|1=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter}}' (the Kaguya-hime legend), often called Japan's oldest narrative, and Kōryō in Kitakatsuragi District, Nara Prefecture, is said to be a strong candidate. '''Reasons Kōryō is said to be the setting''' * The old bamboo cutter's name is '''Sanuki no Miyatsuko''' - opening of the tale: "Long ago there was an old bamboo cutter ... his name was called Sanuki no Miyatsuko." * The old man's name indicates he was the head of the Sanuki lineage living in the Yamato locality called '''Sankichi (Sanuki)'''. * In the Heian-period "Engishiki," the location of the Shikinaisha Sanuki Shrine is written as '''Sankichi-gō, Hirose District, Yamato Province''', corresponding to '''Miyoshi, Kōryō, Kitakatsuragi District, Nara'''. === Film location === [[ファイル:奈良県_広陵町_讃岐神社_ロケ地表示.jpg|サムネイル|Sanuki Shrine filming-location sign]] It was selected as one filming location for the movie '{{ill|lt=Tenshi no Iru Toshokan|Q28943614|WD=Q28943614|1=Library with Angels}},' set in Nara's Katsuragi area ({{ill|lt=Yamatotakada|Q838669|WD=Q838669|1=Yamatotakada, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kashiba|Q696643|WD=Q696643|1=Kashiba, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672|1=Katsuragi, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kōryō|Q1346830|WD=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}}){{Cite web|url=http://www.toshokan-movie.com/|title=Japanese film "Tenshi no Iru Toshokan"|access-date=2017-2-11}}. Starring: {{ill|lt=Koshiba Fūka|Q11463509|WD=Q11463509|1=Fuka Koshiba}}; co-starring: {{ill|lt=Yokohama Ryūsei|Q15876471|WD=Q15876471|1=Ryusei Yokohama}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related item === {{Commonscat|Sanuki-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Umami Kofun Group|Q11668062|WD=Q11668062|1=Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sanuki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sanuki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:Architecture of Koryō Town]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Inbe clan]][[Category:qq]]

Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 別名=Hirose Daimyōjin / Minami Kawai Myōjin | 本殿=sangen-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=hiwadabuki|1=UNKNOWN}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} | 神事= | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Sanuki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sanuki Shrine''' (さぬきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Miyoshi, {{ill|lt=Kōryō|Q1346830|WD=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013|1=Kitakatsuragi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Because the "old bamboo cutter" in '{{ill|lt=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|Q923593|WD=Q923593|1=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter}}' is named "Sanuki no Miyatsuko," the shrine presents itself as one related to the tale, identifying this with Sanuki in Hirose District of Yamato{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Kōryō Town (main volume) |date=2001-5-31 |publisher=Kōryō Town |pages=1125-1139 |author=Kōryō Town}}. === Enshrined deities === Currently it enshrines {{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Mikoto|1=Kunitama}}, Waka-uka-no-me-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}. In '{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}},' entry for {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 7 ({{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355|1=883}}), 12th month, 2nd day, it says "bestow Junior Fifth Lower rank on Sankichi Ōtate-no-Mikoto and Sankichi Inoki deity," thought to refer to this shrine's deities. === History === The place name "Miyoshi" is read "Mitsuyoshi" now, but it was formerly written "Sankichi" and read "Sanuki." This area is thought to have been settled by the {{ill|lt=Imibe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229|lt=Imibe clan|1=Inbe clan}} of {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}, who invited the deity of their homeland and founded the shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Sanuki Shrine, Hirose District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}," ranked as a small shrine. Because Waka-uka-no-me-no-Mikoto of Hirose Kawai (present {{ill|lt=Hirose Taisha|Q705281|WD=Q705281|1=Hirose Taisha}}) was invited, until the Edo period it was called "Hirose Daimyōjin" and "Minami Kawai Myōjin." A fire occurred in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1611|Q6768|WD=Q6768|1=1611}}), first month; the current honden was rebuilt at that time. === Precinct === File:讃岐神社本殿.jpg|Honden File:Sanuki-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii ファイル:讃岐神社境内出土 石製坩 (J-13065・13066).JPG|Stone crucibles excavated in precinct
{{small|{{ill|lt=Tokyo National Museum|Q653433|WD=Q653433|1=Tokyo National Museum}} display.}} === Sanuki Shrine and The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter === There are multiple theories about the setting of '{{ill|lt=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|Q923593|WD=Q923593|1=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter}}' (the Kaguya-hime legend), often called Japan's oldest narrative, and Kōryō in Kitakatsuragi District, Nara Prefecture, is said to be a strong candidate. '''Reasons Kōryō is said to be the setting''' * The old bamboo cutter's name is '''Sanuki no Miyatsuko''' - opening of the tale: "Long ago there was an old bamboo cutter ... his name was called Sanuki no Miyatsuko." * The old man's name indicates he was the head of the Sanuki lineage living in the Yamato locality called '''Sankichi (Sanuki)'''. * In the Heian-period "Engishiki," the location of the Shikinaisha Sanuki Shrine is written as '''Sankichi-gō, Hirose District, Yamato Province''', corresponding to '''Miyoshi, Kōryō, Kitakatsuragi District, Nara'''. === Film location === [[ファイル:奈良県_広陵町_讃岐神社_ロケ地表示.jpg|サムネイル|Sanuki Shrine filming-location sign]] It was selected as one filming location for the movie '{{ill|lt=Tenshi no Iru Toshokan|Q28943614|WD=Q28943614|1=Library with Angels}},' set in Nara's Katsuragi area ({{ill|lt=Yamatotakada|Q838669|WD=Q838669|1=Yamatotakada, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kashiba|Q696643|WD=Q696643|1=Kashiba, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672|1=Katsuragi, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kōryō|Q1346830|WD=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}}){{Cite web|url=http://www.toshokan-movie.com/|title=Japanese film "Tenshi no Iru Toshokan"|access-date=2017-2-11}}. Starring: {{ill|lt=Koshiba Fūka|Q11463509|WD=Q11463509|1=Fuka Koshiba}}; co-starring: {{ill|lt=Yokohama Ryūsei|Q15876471|WD=Q15876471|1=Ryusei Yokohama}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related item === {{Commonscat|Sanuki-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Umami Kofun Group|Q11668062|WD=Q11668062|1=Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sanuki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sanuki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:Architecture of Koryō Town]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Inbe clan]]

Waka-uka-no-me-no-Mikoto
{{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 別名=Hirose Daimyōjin / Minami Kawai Myōjin | 本殿=sangen-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=hiwadabuki|1=UNKNOWN}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} | 神事= | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Sanuki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sanuki Shrine''' (さぬきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Miyoshi, {{ill|lt=Kōryō|Q1346830|WD=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013|1=Kitakatsuragi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Because the "old bamboo cutter" in '{{ill|lt=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|Q923593|WD=Q923593|1=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter}}' is named "Sanuki no Miyatsuko," the shrine presents itself as one related to the tale, identifying this with Sanuki in Hirose District of Yamato{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Kōryō Town (main volume) |date=2001-5-31 |publisher=Kōryō Town |pages=1125-1139 |author=Kōryō Town}}. === Enshrined deities === Currently it enshrines {{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Mikoto|1=Kunitama}}, Waka-uka-no-me-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}. In '{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}},' entry for {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 7 ({{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355|1=883}}), 12th month, 2nd day, it says "bestow Junior Fifth Lower rank on Sankichi Ōtate-no-Mikoto and Sankichi Inoki deity," thought to refer to this shrine's deities. === History === The place name "Miyoshi" is read "Mitsuyoshi" now, but it was formerly written "Sankichi" and read "Sanuki." This area is thought to have been settled by the {{ill|lt=Imibe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229|lt=Imibe clan|1=Inbe clan}} of {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}, who invited the deity of their homeland and founded the shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Sanuki Shrine, Hirose District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}," ranked as a small shrine. Because Waka-uka-no-me-no-Mikoto of Hirose Kawai (present {{ill|lt=Hirose Taisha|Q705281|WD=Q705281|1=Hirose Taisha}}) was invited, until the Edo period it was called "Hirose Daimyōjin" and "Minami Kawai Myōjin." A fire occurred in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1611|Q6768|WD=Q6768|1=1611}}), first month; the current honden was rebuilt at that time. === Precinct === File:讃岐神社本殿.jpg|Honden File:Sanuki-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii ファイル:讃岐神社境内出土 石製坩 (J-13065・13066).JPG|Stone crucibles excavated in precinct
{{small|{{ill|lt=Tokyo National Museum|Q653433|WD=Q653433|1=Tokyo National Museum}} display.}} === Sanuki Shrine and The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter === There are multiple theories about the setting of '{{ill|lt=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|Q923593|WD=Q923593|1=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter}}' (the Kaguya-hime legend), often called Japan's oldest narrative, and Kōryō in Kitakatsuragi District, Nara Prefecture, is said to be a strong candidate. '''Reasons Kōryō is said to be the setting''' * The old bamboo cutter's name is '''Sanuki no Miyatsuko''' - opening of the tale: "Long ago there was an old bamboo cutter ... his name was called Sanuki no Miyatsuko." * The old man's name indicates he was the head of the Sanuki lineage living in the Yamato locality called '''Sankichi (Sanuki)'''. * In the Heian-period "Engishiki," the location of the Shikinaisha Sanuki Shrine is written as '''Sankichi-gō, Hirose District, Yamato Province''', corresponding to '''Miyoshi, Kōryō, Kitakatsuragi District, Nara'''. === Film location === [[ファイル:奈良県_広陵町_讃岐神社_ロケ地表示.jpg|サムネイル|Sanuki Shrine filming-location sign]] It was selected as one filming location for the movie '{{ill|lt=Tenshi no Iru Toshokan|Q28943614|WD=Q28943614|1=Library with Angels}},' set in Nara's Katsuragi area ({{ill|lt=Yamatotakada|Q838669|WD=Q838669|1=Yamatotakada, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kashiba|Q696643|WD=Q696643|1=Kashiba, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672|1=Katsuragi, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kōryō|Q1346830|WD=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}}){{Cite web|url=http://www.toshokan-movie.com/|title=Japanese film "Tenshi no Iru Toshokan"|access-date=2017-2-11}}. Starring: {{ill|lt=Koshiba Fūka|Q11463509|WD=Q11463509|1=Fuka Koshiba}}; co-starring: {{ill|lt=Yokohama Ryūsei|Q15876471|WD=Q15876471|1=Ryusei Yokohama}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related item === {{Commonscat|Sanuki-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Umami Kofun Group|Q11668062|WD=Q11668062|1=Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sanuki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sanuki Shrine]][[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:Architecture of Koryō Town]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Inbe clan]]
